channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Hollywood heavyweights oppose Paramount deal
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with TV writer and showrunner Damon Lindelof about the open letter he and thousands of others signed, opposing Paramount's takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with TV writer and showrunner Damon Lindelof about the open letter he and thousands of others signed, opposing Paramount's takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
item.description: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.
item.content:encoded:
The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

(Image credit: Peter Morrison)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The labor economics of 'Alien' — and its lessons for inequality on Earth
item.description: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.content:encoded: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: 2 local TV giants merged. Then a court stepped in
item.description: Local TV giant Nexstar's $6.2 billion deal to acquire rival Tegna won speedy approval from Trump administration regulators. But it faces a tough challenge from a pair of antitrust lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
Local TV giant Nexstar's $6.2 billion deal to acquire rival Tegna won speedy approval from Trump administration regulators. But it faces a tough challenge from a pair of antitrust lawsuits.

(Image credit: Brendan Carr)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: How AI is getting better at finding security holes
item.description: Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.
item.content:encoded:
Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly 2 years as energy costs spike
item.description: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.
item.content:encoded:
Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Women are getting most of the new jobs. What's going on with men?
item.description: Over the past year, the vast majority of new jobs have gone to women. One economist says to help men find work, we need to embrace ways to "make girly jobs appeal to manly men."
item.content:encoded:
Over the past year, the vast majority of new jobs have gone to women. One economist says to help men find work, we need to embrace ways to "make girly jobs appeal to manly men."

(Image credit: melitas)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: How governments have tried to hide information about the Iran war online
item.description: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.
item.content:encoded:
Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

(Image credit: Planet)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Inflation climbs to highest level in nearly 2 years
item.description: The federal government delivered a cost-of-living report Friday. A spike in gasoline prices triggered by the war with Iran contributed to inflation reaching its highest level in nearly two years.
item.content:encoded: The federal government delivered a cost-of-living report Friday. A spike in gasoline prices triggered by the war with Iran contributed to inflation reaching its highest level in nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Well-timed bets on Polymarket tied to the Iran war draw calls for investigations from lawmakers
item.description: Calls are increasing inside Congress for investigations into the prediction market platform Polymarket after the latest instance where groups of anonymous traders made strategic, well-timed bets on a major geopolitical event hours before it occurred.
item.content:encoded:
Calls are increasing inside Congress for investigations into the prediction market platform Polymarket after the latest instance where groups of anonymous traders made strategic, well-timed bets on a major geopolitical event hours before it occurred.

(Image credit: Amanda Andrade-Rhoades)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
